message:
Add caut-CAAM integration coordinator for proactive account switching

Implements bd-384s3: coordinator.go bridges caut and CAAM to trigger
proactive account switches when API usage hits 90% threshold, avoiding
actual rate limits. Includes switch cooldowns, callbacks, and alerts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-2lsyy","title":"caut Integration: Usage Tracking and Quota Visibility","description":"Integrate caut (coding_agent_usage_tracker) for real-time quota and spend tracking across all agents.

## Project Location
/dp/coding_agent_usage_tracker (Rust CLI)

## Problem Being Solved

Without visibility into API usage:
- Surprise bills at end of month
- Agents hit limits unexpectedly
- No way to budget across projects
- Can't see which agents are most expensive

## What caut Does

caut monitors LLM provider usage across 16+ providers:
- Claude (claude.ai, API)
- OpenAI (ChatGPT, API)
- Gemini (web, API)
- Anthropic Console
- Many more

### Data Sources
- Browser extension intercepts
- API response headers
- CLI tool output parsing
- Provider dashboard scraping

### Key caut Commands
```bash
caut status              # Current usage summary
caut status --json       # JSON output for agents
caut history --today     # Today's usage breakdown
caut cost --month        # Monthly cost projection
caut alerts set 80       # Alert at 80% of limits
```

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### 1. Dashboard Widget
Main use case: see usage at a glance in NTM dashboard
- Per-provider usage bars
- Cost accumulator (daily/monthly)
- Alert indicators when approaching limits

### 2. Robot Mode Commands
- --robot-quota-status: Get all quota info as JSON
- --robot-quota-check <provider>: Check specific provider

### 3. Proactive Alerts
When caut reports >80% usage:
- Highlight in dashboard (red/yellow)
- Consider triggering CAAM rotation if enabled

### 4. Per-Agent Attribution
If possible, tag API calls with agent/pane ID for:
- Per-agent cost breakdown
- Identify expensive agents
- Budget allocation

## Considerations
- caut runs independently, NTM just reads its output
- Polling frequency: every 60 seconds
- caut may have stale data (depends on data sources)
- Don't duplicate alerting if CAAM also tracking","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:56:25.641626929Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T06:56:25.714579941Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lsyy","depends_on_id":"bd-mk1i5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:56:25.712930134Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2mgl","title":"Unit Tests: Context monitoring and injection","description":"## Comprehensive Unit Test Suite for Proactive Context Management

### Test Coverage Required
1. **ContextMonitor tests** (internal/context/monitor_test.go)
   - Context length estimation
   - Threshold detection (50%, 75%, 90%)

   - Token budget constraints

3. **ContextEstimator tests** (internal/context/estimate_test.go)

### Test Data
- Sample scrollbacks at various lengths
- Edge cases: empty, very long, binary content

### Logging

 {"id":"bd-2n4v","title":"Task: Integrate predictor with dashboard","description":"## Purpose
Display context prediction status in the NTM dashboard.

## Dashboard Component
Context Status Display:
⚠️ myproject__cc_1: Context at 72% with high output velocity
   Estimated exhaustion in 8 minutes
   [Compact Now] [Remind in 5m] [Ignore]

## Integration Points
- Poll context predictor periodically (every 10s)
- Update dashboard status when warning threshold crossed
- Show predicted time remaining
- Offer action buttons for proactive compaction

## Color Coding
- Green: Usage < 60% or low velocity
- Yellow: Usage 60-75% or warning triggered
- Red: Usage > 75% or compact triggered

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dashboard shows context status per agent pane
- Visual warning when approaching limit
- Time-to-exhaustion displayed accurately
- Actions accessible via keyboard shortcuts

## Technical Notes
Use existing dashboard patterns in internal/tui/

 {"id":"bd-377a","title":"Feature: Manual review-queue prompt generator","description":"## Overview
Provide a **manual** review-queue command that suggests review prompts for idle agents, enabling efficient code review workflows without automatic commit detection.

## User Story
As a user with multiple agents completing work, I want to queue up review tasks for idle agents so that agents are always productively working on something valuable.

## Scope
- `ntm review-queue <session>` lists idle agents + recommended review prompts
- Optional `--send` to dispatch prompts after confirmation
- Optional `--format json` for robot mode integration
- Integrates with assignment tracking to target agents not currently assigned

## Idle Detection
An agent is considered "idle" when:
- Status is not "working" or "waiting_for_input"
- Last activity was more than `--idle-threshold` (default: 2 minutes) ago
- Agent has no pending prompt being processed

```
Idle Agents (3):
  cc_1 - idle for 5m, last task: "Implement auth"
  cc_2 - idle for 3m, last task: "Fix database bug"
  cod_1 - idle for 8m, last task: "Write tests"
```

## Review Prompt Generation
Review prompts are generated based on:
1. **Completed work by other agents** - "Review the changes to auth.go made by cc_3"
2. **Git commits since session start** - "Review commit abc123: 'Add user validation'"
3. **Custom review templates** - User-defined review prompt templates

### Prompt Templates
```yaml
# In ~/.config/ntm/review-templates.yaml
code_review:
  template: "Review the following code changes for correctness, security, and style: {diff}"
  applies_to: ["*.go", "*.ts", "*.py"]

security_review:
  template: "Perform a security review of: {file}. Look for injection, auth bypass, data exposure."
  applies_to: ["**/auth/**", "**/api/**"]
```

## Output Format
### Human Format (default)
```
Review Queue for 'myproject':

Idle Agents: 3
Pending Reviews: 5

Suggested Assignments:
  1. cc_1 <- Review auth.go changes (by cc_3)
  2. cc_2 <- Review database.go changes (by cod_1)
  3. cod_1 <- Security review of api/users.go

Send these prompts? (y/n/edit)
```

### Robot Format (--format json)
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "idle_agents": ["cc_1", "cc_2", "cod_1"],
  "suggested_reviews": [
    {"agent": "cc_1", "prompt": "Review auth.go...", "source": "agent_work"},
    {"agent": "cc_2", "prompt": "Review database.go...", "source": "agent_work"}
  ]
}
```

## CLI Examples
```bash
# Show review queue (no action)
ntm review-queue myproject

# Show queue and send after confirmation
ntm review-queue myproject --send

# Custom idle threshold
ntm review-queue myproject --idle-threshold 5m

# Robot mode for scripting
ntm review-queue myproject --format json

# Only show queue for Claude agents
ntm review-queue myproject --filter cc
```

## Non‑Goals
- No automatic commit detection (manual only)
- No background daemon
- No automatic dispatch without confirmation
- No integration with external CI/CD (yet)

## Implementation Notes
- Query tmux pane status to detect idle state
- Parse session history to find completed work
- Use git log to find recent commits
- Match review templates to changed files

## Tests
- Unit: idle agent detection logic
- Unit: prompt template parsing and variable substitution
- Unit: review source detection (agent work vs git commits)
- E2E: review-queue → confirm → send → verify delivery
- E2E: robot mode output format validation

## Logging
```go
log.Printf("[E2E-REVIEWQ] session=%s idle_count=%d review_count=%d", session, idle, reviews)
log.Printf("[E2E-REVIEWQ] agent=%s idle_duration=%s last_task=%q", agent, duration, task)
log.Printf("[E2E-REVIEWQ] sent=%d skipped=%d errors=%d", sent, skipped, errors)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Deterministic prompt list for same state
- [ ] Safe confirmation before sending
- [ ] Works with/without assignment tracking
- [ ] Idle threshold is configurable
- [ ] Review templates are customizable
- [ ] Robot mode outputs valid JSON
- [ ] Integrates with git for commit-based reviews
- [ ] Gracefully handles no idle agents scenario","notes":"Rescoped from auto-commit trigger to manual review-queue to avoid over-engineering.\n\n## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:39:33.973123397Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T21:38:58.605133331Z","close_reason":"Over-engineering: automatic review queue requires commit detection, idle detection, and prompt generation. Manual review orchestration is more controllable and transparent.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-377a","depends_on_id":"bd-10ufs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:13.736589407Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-377a","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:38:58.605107983Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3822","title":"Add comprehensive TUI debug mode with state inspection","description":"## Overview
Add a comprehensive debug mode to the TUI that helps diagnose rendering issues, state problems, and performance bottlenecks.

## Activation
- Environment variable: \`NTM_TUI_DEBUG=1\`
- Command flag: \`ntm dashboard --debug\`
- Runtime toggle: Press \`ctrl+d\` in dashboard

## Debug Features

### 1. State Inspector Panel
Show current model state in a collapsible panel:
\`\`\`
┌─ Debug State ─────────────────────────────────┐
│ Terminal: 120x40 (tier: TierSplit)            │
│ Content Height: 30 (header:6, footer:4)       │
│ Panes: 17 (claude:16, codex:0, gemini:1)      │
│ Active Panel: PanelActivity (cursor: 3)       │
│ Refresh: 1.2s ago (paused: false)             │
│ Fetching: session:no context:no alerts:no    │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

### 2. Render Timing
Track and display render performance:
\`\`\`
┌─ Render Timing ───────────────────────────────┐
│ Last View(): 12.3ms                           │
│ Last Update(): 0.8ms                          │
│ Frames/sec: 4.2                               │
│ View() calls: 847                             │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

### 3. Tea Log Integration
Enable Bubble Tea's built-in logging:
\`\`\`go
if os.Getenv("NTM_TUI_DEBUG") != "" {
    f, _ := tea.LogToFile("debug.log", "debug")
    defer f.Close()
}
\`\`\`

### 4. Content Overflow Detection
Highlight when content exceeds available space:
\`\`\`go
func (m Model) View() string {
    content := m.buildView()
    
    if m.debug {
        lines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
        if len(lines) > m.height {
            // Add warning overlay
            content = m.addOverflowWarning(content, len(lines), m.height)
        }
    }
    
    return content
}
\`\`\`

### 5. ANSI Code Visualization
Option to show ANSI codes visually:
\`\`\`
Normal: [bold red]Hello[reset]
Debug:  <ESC>[1;31mHello<ESC>[0m
\`\`\`

## Implementation

### Debug Model Fields
\`\`\`go
type Model struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    // Debug mode
    debug           bool
    debugPanel      *DebugPanel
    renderTimes     []time.Duration
    viewCallCount   int
    lastViewTime    time.Time
    lastUpdateTime  time.Time
}
\`\`\`

### Debug Panel Component
\`\`\`go
// internal/tui/components/debug.go
type DebugPanel struct {
    width, height int
    state         map[string]string
    timing        TimingStats
    collapsed     bool
}

func (p *DebugPanel) View() string {
    if p.collapsed {
        return "  [ctrl+d to expand debug]"
    }
    
    var b strings.Builder
    // ... render debug info ...
    return lipgloss.NewStyle().
        Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
        BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("208")). // Orange for debug
        Render(b.String())
}
\`\`\`

### Key Bindings
\`\`\`go
case tea.KeyMsg:
    if m.debug {
        switch msg.String() {
        case "ctrl+d":
            m.debugPanel.collapsed = !m.debugPanel.collapsed
        case "ctrl+l":
            m.debugPanel.LogCurrentState()
        }
    }
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] NTM_TUI_DEBUG=1 enables debug mode
- [ ] Debug panel shows current state
- [ ] Render timing displayed
- [ ] Content overflow warnings shown
- [ ] tea.LogToFile creates debug.log
- [ ] ctrl+d toggles debug panel

## Unit Tests
1. TestDebugMode_Activation - env var enables debug
2. TestDebugPanel_StateDisplay - state rendered correctly
3. TestDebugPanel_Toggle - ctrl+d toggles visibility
4. TestDebugMode_TimingTracking - render times recorded

## Logging
Debug mode should log:
- Every WindowSizeMsg with dimensions
- Every View() call with duration
- State changes with before/after
- Panel focus changes
- Fetch operations with timing","notes":"Implemented NTM_TUI_DEBUG environment variable support (in dashboardDebugEnabled function, line 3477). Added --debug flag for dashboard command. Added ctrl+d as additional key binding for diagnostics toggle alongside existing 'd' key. Existing diagnostics panel already shows state info. Additional features like render timing, tea.LogToFile, and content overflow detection can be added in future iterations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:12:25.773408646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:12:33.654506292Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:12:33.654347473Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3822","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:31.531090602Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-384s3","title":"caut: Implement CAAM integration for coordinated alerts","description":"Coordinate caut alerts with CAAM for automatic account switching.

## Background

Both caut and CAAM track usage/limits. Need to avoid duplication and coordinate:
- caut: historical tracking, cost visibility, projections
- CAAM: real-time rate limit response, account switching

## Integration Points

### 1. Unified Alert Source
Use caut as the primary source for proactive alerts.
CAAM handles reactive (actual rate limit hit).

### 2. Pre-emptive Switching
When caut reports >90% usage for a provider:
1. Alert user via dashboard
2. Optionally trigger CAAM switch before hitting actual limit
3. Avoid the rate limit entirely

### 3. Data Sharing
Consider feeding caut data to CAAM for:
- Better account selection (pick account with most headroom)
- Cooldown predictions
- Cost optimization across accounts

## Implementation

```go
func (m *IntegrationManager) OnCautAlert(provider string, usagePercent int) {
    if usagePercent >= 90 && m.config.CAAM.Enabled && m.config.CAAM.AutoRotate {
        // Proactive switch before hitting limit
        m.caam.SwitchAccount(provider)
    }
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/integrations/coordinator.go

## Considerations
- Don't switch if user just switched manually
- Respect cooldowns from CAAM
- Log all proactive switches for audit","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:59:56.277818904Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:01:01.736219773Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-384s3","depends_on_id":"bd-26185","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:01:01.736182332Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-384s3","depends_on_id":"bd-2dyqk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:00:56.807551922Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-384s3","depends_on_id":"bd-2lsyy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:59:56.307395295Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-384s3","title":"caut: Implement CAAM integration for coordinated alerts","description":"Coordinate caut alerts with CAAM for automatic account switching.

## Background

Both caut and CAAM track usage/limits. Need to avoid duplication and coordinate:
- caut: historical tracking, cost visibility, projections
- CAAM: real-time rate limit response, account switching

## Integration Points

### 1. Unified Alert Source
Use caut as the primary source for proactive alerts.
CAAM handles reactive (actual rate limit hit).

### 2. Pre-emptive Switching
When caut reports >90% usage for a provider:
1. Alert user via dashboard
2. Optionally trigger CAAM switch before hitting actual limit
3. Avoid the rate limit entirely

### 3. Data Sharing
Consider feeding caut data to CAAM for:
- Better account selection (pick account with most headroom)
- Cooldown predictions
- Cost optimization across accounts

## Implementation

```go
func (m *IntegrationManager) OnCautAlert(provider string, usagePercent int) {
    if usagePercent >= 90 && m.config.CAAM.Enabled && m.config.CAAM.AutoRotate {
        // Proactive switch before hitting limit
        m.caam.SwitchAccount(provider)
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-38gl","title":"EPIC: One-Command Project Initialization (ntm init)","description":"**IDEA #2 - FOUNDATIONAL (93% confidence)**

## What It Does
A single command transforms any directory into a fully-configured NTM project with all flywheel integrations ready to use.

## Why This Matters
- First impressions determine adoption
- Currently: User must manually create .ntm/, .beads/, register with Agent Mail, etc.
- After: One command, everything works
- Reduces 'time to first successful spawn' from 10+ minutes to 30 seconds

## Implementation Scope
Creates: .ntm/config.toml, .ntm/personas.toml, .ntm/templates/, .ntm/pipelines/, .beads/, AGENTS.md
Configures: Agent Mail registration, Git hooks, Default personas, Project-specific config

## Flywheel Alignment
- Bootstrap: Sets up all flywheel integrations from day one
- Users get value from Agent Mail, BV, etc. immediately
- Without this, many users never discover integrations exist

## Technical Notes
- New file: internal/cli/init.go
- Template-specific AGENTS.md generation based on project type (Go, Python, Node, Rust)
- Interactive mode with Bubble Tea wizard
- Idempotent: safe to run multiple times

 {"id":"bd-38ik","title":"E2E Tests: CM workflow with real subprocess","description":"## End-to-End Test Suite for CM Integration

### E2E Test Scenarios
1. **Full CM integration flow**
   - Start ntm spawn with CM enabled
   - Verify cm recall called on spawn
   - Verify context injected into agents
   - Verify guards included

2. **Memory persistence across sessions**
   - Spawn session, write memory
   - Kill session
   - Spawn new session
   - Verify memory recalled

3. **Guard effectiveness**
   - Set up a guard (e.g., 'never rm -rf /')
   - Spawn agent
   - Verify guard in agent context
   - (Optional) Test guard prevents dangerous action

4. **Multi-agent memory coordination**
   - Spawn 3 agents
   - Each should have project-specific context
   - Verify no memory conflicts

### Detailed Logging

log.Printf("[E2E-CM] Recall returned %d memories", len(memories))
log.Printf("[E2E-CM] Injecting context: %d tokens", tokenCount)

 {"id":"bd-38kx","title":"EPIC: Smart Session Recovery with Full Context Continuity","description":"**IDEA #1 - HIGHEST IMPACT (95% confidence)**

## What It Does
When NTM spawns agents—whether after a crash, reboot, manual restart, or returning after hours/days—it automatically reconstructs the context each agent needs to continue exactly where it left off.

## Why This Is The Best Idea
- The #1 frustration in multi-agent workflows is context loss
- Every session restart currently feels like starting over
- This transforms restarts from painful to seamless
- Users will feel like their agents have 'memory'

## How It Works
On session spawn/respawn:
1. Check for prior state: Look for existing checkpoints, Agent Mail threads, and session history
2. Query Agent Mail: Fetch the last N messages from threads this session participated in
3. Query CM (CASS Memory): Retrieve relevant procedural memories for this project
4. Query BV: Get current in-progress beads assigned to this session
5. Synthesize recovery prompt: Construct a structured 'here's what you were doing' context injection
6. Auto-send on spawn: Each agent receives this context automatically before user's first prompt

## Flywheel Alignment: Perfect
- Integrates checkpoints (already built)
- Integrates Agent Mail (already built)
- Integrates CM (part of flywheel)
- Integrates BV (part of flywheel)
- Each use makes recovery context richer

## Technical Implementation
- Changes to spawn.go: buildRecoveryContext() function
- New config options under [recovery] section
- Graceful degradation: works with any subset of integrations available

## Dependencies
Depends on: ntm init (for proper project setup)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:21:01.027989391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:43:49.544438840Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:43:49.544326338Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38kx","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:06:06.298381905Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,439 @@
+// Package integrations provides coordination between external tool integrations.
+package integrations
+
+import (
+	"context"
+	"log/slog"
+	"sync"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/alerts"
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/config"
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/integrations/caut"
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/tools"
+)
+
+var coordinatorLogger = slog.Default().With("component", "integrations.coordinator")
+
+// Coordinator manages interactions between caut and CAAM integrations.
+// It monitors caut alerts for high usage and proactively triggers
+// CAAM account switches before hitting actual rate limits.
+type Coordinator struct {
+	mu sync.RWMutex
+
+	caamAdapter  *tools.CAAMAdapter
+	cautPoller   *caut.UsagePoller
+	alertTracker *alerts.Tracker
+	config       *CoordinatorConfig
+
+	// Track recent switches to prevent thrashing
+	lastSwitch     map[string]time.Time // provider -> last switch time
+	switchCooldown time.Duration
+
+	// Callbacks for switch events
+	onSwitch SwitchCallback
+
+	stopCh chan struct{}
+	doneCh chan struct{}
+
+	running bool
+}
+
+// CoordinatorConfig holds configuration for the coordinator.
+type CoordinatorConfig struct {
+	// CAAMEnabled indicates if CAAM integration is available
+	CAAMEnabled bool
+	// CautEnabled indicates if caut integration is available
+	CautEnabled bool
+	// AutoRotate enables automatic CAAM rotation on high caut usage
+	AutoRotate bool
+	// ProactiveThreshold is the usage percentage to trigger pre-emptive switch (0-100)
+	ProactiveThreshold float64
+	// SwitchCooldown is minimum time between switches for the same provider
+	SwitchCooldown time.Duration
+	// CheckInterval is how often to check for alerts
+	CheckInterval time.Duration
+}
+
+// DefaultCoordinatorConfig returns sensible defaults.
+func DefaultCoordinatorConfig() CoordinatorConfig {
+	return CoordinatorConfig{
+		CAAMEnabled:        true,
+		CautEnabled:        true,
+		AutoRotate:         true,
+		ProactiveThreshold: 90.0, // Switch at 90% to avoid hitting limit
+		SwitchCooldown:     5 * time.Minute,
+		CheckInterval:      30 * time.Second,
+	}
+}
+
+// SwitchCallback is called when an account switch occurs.
+type SwitchCallback func(event SwitchEvent)
+
+// SwitchEvent contains details about a proactive account switch.
+type SwitchEvent struct {
+	Provider         string    `json:"provider"`
+	UsagePercent     float64   `json:"usage_percent"`
+	Reason           string    `json:"reason"` // "proactive" or "rate_limit"
+	PreviousAccount  string    `json:"previous_account,omitempty"`
+	NewAccount       string    `json:"new_account,omitempty"`
+	Success          bool      `json:"success"`
+	Error            string    `json:"error,omitempty"`
+	Timestamp        time.Time `json:"timestamp"`
+	AccountsRemaining int      `json:"accounts_remaining,omitempty"`
+}
+
+// NewCoordinator creates a new integration coordinator.
+func NewCoordinator(cfg *CoordinatorConfig) *Coordinator {
+	if cfg == nil {
+		defaultCfg := DefaultCoordinatorConfig()
+		cfg = &defaultCfg
+	}
+
+	return &Coordinator{
+		caamAdapter:    tools.NewCAAMAdapter(),
+		cautPoller:     caut.GetGlobalPoller(),
+		alertTracker:   alerts.GetGlobalTracker(),
+		config:         cfg,
+		lastSwitch:     make(map[string]time.Time),
+		switchCooldown: cfg.SwitchCooldown,
+		stopCh:         make(chan struct{}),
+		doneCh:         make(chan struct{}),
+	}
+}
+
+// NewCoordinatorFromConfig creates a coordinator from NTM config.
+func NewCoordinatorFromConfig(intCfg *config.IntegrationsConfig) *Coordinator {
+	cfg := CoordinatorConfig{
+		CAAMEnabled:        intCfg.CAAM.Enabled,
+		CautEnabled:        intCfg.Caut.Enabled,
+		AutoRotate:         intCfg.CAAM.AutoRotate,
+		ProactiveThreshold: 90.0, // Fixed at 90% for proactive switching
+		SwitchCooldown:     time.Duration(intCfg.CAAM.AccountCooldown) * time.Second,
+		CheckInterval:      30 * time.Second,
+	}
+
+	return NewCoordinator(&cfg)
+}
+
+// SetSwitchCallback sets a callback to be invoked on account switches.
+func (c *Coordinator) SetSwitchCallback(cb SwitchCallback) {
+	c.mu.Lock()
+	defer c.mu.Unlock()
+	c.onSwitch = cb
+}
+
+// Start begins monitoring for proactive switching opportunities.
+func (c *Coordinator) Start() error {
+	c.mu.Lock()
+	defer c.mu.Unlock()
+
+	if c.running {
+		return nil
+	}
+
+	// Verify both integrations are available
+	if !c.config.CAAMEnabled || !c.config.CautEnabled {
+		coordinatorLogger.Info("coordinator not starting: integrations not enabled",
+			"caam_enabled", c.config.CAAMEnabled,
+			"caut_enabled", c.config.CautEnabled,
+		)
+		return nil
+	}
+
+	// Check if CAAM is actually available
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
+	defer cancel()
+
+	if !c.caamAdapter.IsAvailable(ctx) {
+		coordinatorLogger.Info("coordinator not starting: CAAM not available")
+		return nil
+	}
+
+	if !c.caamAdapter.HasMultipleAccounts(ctx) {
+		coordinatorLogger.Info("coordinator not starting: CAAM has only one account (no rotation possible)")
+		return nil
+	}
+
+	c.running = true
+	c.stopCh = make(chan struct{})
+	c.doneCh = make(chan struct{})
+
+	go c.monitorLoop()
+
+	coordinatorLogger.Info("caut-CAAM coordinator started",
+		"proactive_threshold", c.config.ProactiveThreshold,
+		"auto_rotate", c.config.AutoRotate,
+	)
+
+	return nil
+}
+
+// Stop halts the coordinator.
+func (c *Coordinator) Stop() {
+	c.mu.Lock()
+	if !c.running {
+		c.mu.Unlock()
+		return
+	}
+	close(c.stopCh)
+	c.mu.Unlock()
+
+	<-c.doneCh
+
+	c.mu.Lock()
+	c.running = false
+	c.mu.Unlock()
+
+	coordinatorLogger.Info("caut-CAAM coordinator stopped")
+}
+
+// IsRunning returns true if the coordinator is active.
+func (c *Coordinator) IsRunning() bool {
+	c.mu.RLock()
+	defer c.mu.RUnlock()
+	return c.running
+}
+
+// OnCautAlert handles alerts from the caut poller.
+// This is the main entry point for proactive switching.
+func (c *Coordinator) OnCautAlert(provider string, usagePercent float64) {
+	c.mu.RLock()
+	autoRotate := c.config.AutoRotate
+	threshold := c.config.ProactiveThreshold
+	c.mu.RUnlock()
+
+	if !autoRotate {
+		coordinatorLogger.Debug("auto-rotate disabled, skipping proactive switch",
+			"provider", provider,
+			"usage_percent", usagePercent,
+		)
+		return
+	}
+
+	if usagePercent < threshold {
+		return // Not at threshold yet
+	}
+
+	// Check cooldown
+	if c.inCooldown(provider) {
+		coordinatorLogger.Debug("provider in cooldown, skipping switch",
+			"provider", provider,
+			"usage_percent", usagePercent,
+		)
+		return
+	}
+
+	// Trigger proactive switch
+	c.triggerSwitch(provider, usagePercent, "proactive")
+}
+
+// triggerSwitch performs the actual account switch.
+func (c *Coordinator) triggerSwitch(provider string, usagePercent float64, reason string) {
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
+	defer cancel()
+
+	event := SwitchEvent{
+		Provider:     provider,
+		UsagePercent: usagePercent,
+		Reason:       reason,
+		Timestamp:    time.Now(),
+	}
+
+	coordinatorLogger.Info("triggering proactive account switch",
+		"provider", provider,
+		"usage_percent", usagePercent,
+		"reason", reason,
+	)
+
+	// Perform the switch
+	result, err := c.caamAdapter.SwitchToNextAccount(ctx, provider)
+	if err != nil {
+		event.Success = false
+		event.Error = err.Error()
+		coordinatorLogger.Warn("proactive account switch failed",
+			"provider", provider,
+			"error", err,
+		)
+
+		// Add alert for failed switch
+		c.alertTracker.AddAlert(alerts.Alert{
+			ID:       "caut-caam-switch-failed-" + provider,
+			Type:     alerts.AlertRateLimit,
+			Severity: alerts.SeverityWarning,
+			Source:   "coordinator",
+			Message:  "Proactive account switch failed for " + provider + ": " + err.Error(),
+			Context: map[string]interface{}{
+				"provider":      provider,
+				"usage_percent": usagePercent,
+				"reason":        reason,
+			},
+		})
+	} else {
+		event.Success = true
+		event.PreviousAccount = result.PreviousAccount
+		event.NewAccount = result.NewAccount
+		event.AccountsRemaining = result.AccountsRemaining
+
+		// Record switch time for cooldown tracking
+		c.recordSwitch(provider)
+
+		coordinatorLogger.Info("proactive account switch successful",
+			"provider", provider,
+			"previous_account", result.PreviousAccount,
+			"new_account", result.NewAccount,
+			"accounts_remaining", result.AccountsRemaining,
+		)
+
+		// Add informational alert for successful switch
+		c.alertTracker.AddAlert(alerts.Alert{
+			ID:       "caut-caam-switch-" + provider,
+			Type:     alerts.AlertQuotaWarning,
+			Severity: alerts.SeverityInfo,
+			Source:   "coordinator",
+			Message:  "Proactively switched " + provider + " account from " + result.PreviousAccount + " to " + result.NewAccount,
+			Context: map[string]interface{}{
+				"provider":           provider,
+				"usage_percent":      usagePercent,
+				"previous_account":   result.PreviousAccount,
+				"new_account":        result.NewAccount,
+				"accounts_remaining": result.AccountsRemaining,
+			},
+		})
+	}
+
+	// Invoke callback if set
+	c.mu.RLock()
+	cb := c.onSwitch
+	c.mu.RUnlock()
+
+	if cb != nil {
+		cb(event)
+	}
+}
+
+// inCooldown checks if a provider is in switch cooldown.
+func (c *Coordinator) inCooldown(provider string) bool {
+	c.mu.RLock()
+	defer c.mu.RUnlock()
+
+	lastTime, ok := c.lastSwitch[provider]
+	if !ok {
+		return false
+	}
+
+	return time.Since(lastTime) < c.switchCooldown
+}
+
+// recordSwitch records when a switch occurred for cooldown tracking.
+func (c *Coordinator) recordSwitch(provider string) {
+	c.mu.Lock()
+	defer c.mu.Unlock()
+	c.lastSwitch[provider] = time.Now()
+}
+
+// monitorLoop periodically checks caut data for high usage.
+func (c *Coordinator) monitorLoop() {
+	defer close(c.doneCh)
+
+	ticker := time.NewTicker(c.config.CheckInterval)
+	defer ticker.Stop()
+
+	for {
+		select {
+		case <-ticker.C:
+			c.checkUsageForProactiveSwitch()
+
+		case <-c.stopCh:
+			return
+		}
+	}
+}
+
+// checkUsageForProactiveSwitch examines cached caut data for high usage.
+func (c *Coordinator) checkUsageForProactiveSwitch() {
+	cache := c.cautPoller.GetCache()
+	if cache == nil {
+		return
+	}
+
+	status := cache.GetStatus()
+	if status == nil {
+		return
+	}
+
+	threshold := c.config.ProactiveThreshold
+
+	// Check per-provider quotas
+	for _, provider := range status.Providers {
+		if !provider.Enabled || !provider.HasQuota {
+			continue
+		}
+
+		if provider.QuotaUsed >= threshold {
+			c.OnCautAlert(provider.Name, provider.QuotaUsed)
+		}
+	}
+}
+
+// GetLastSwitchTime returns when the last switch occurred for a provider.
+func (c *Coordinator) GetLastSwitchTime(provider string) (time.Time, bool) {
+	c.mu.RLock()
+	defer c.mu.RUnlock()
+
+	t, ok := c.lastSwitch[provider]
+	return t, ok
+}
+
+// ResetCooldown clears the cooldown for a provider (for testing or manual override).
+func (c *Coordinator) ResetCooldown(provider string) {
+	c.mu.Lock()
+	defer c.mu.Unlock()
+	delete(c.lastSwitch, provider)
+}
+
+// Global coordinator instance
+
+var (
+	globalCoordinator     *Coordinator
+	globalCoordinatorOnce sync.Once
+	globalCoordinatorMu   sync.RWMutex
+)
+
+// GetGlobalCoordinator returns the global coordinator singleton.
+func GetGlobalCoordinator() *Coordinator {
+	globalCoordinatorOnce.Do(func() {
+		cfg := DefaultCoordinatorConfig()
+		globalCoordinator = NewCoordinator(&cfg)
+	})
+	return globalCoordinator
+}
+
+// InitGlobalCoordinator initializes the global coordinator with specific config.
+func InitGlobalCoordinator(cfg *CoordinatorConfig) *Coordinator {
+	globalCoordinatorMu.Lock()
+	defer globalCoordinatorMu.Unlock()
+
+	if globalCoordinator != nil {
+		globalCoordinator.Stop()
+	}
+
+	globalCoordinator = NewCoordinator(cfg)
+	return globalCoordinator
+}
+
+// StartGlobalCoordinator starts the global coordinator if not already running.
+func StartGlobalCoordinator() error {
+	return GetGlobalCoordinator().Start()
+}
+
+// StopGlobalCoordinator stops the global coordinator if running.
+func StopGlobalCoordinator() {
+	globalCoordinatorMu.RLock()
+	coord := globalCoordinator
+	globalCoordinatorMu.RUnlock()
+
+	if coord != nil {
+		coord.Stop()
+	}
+}
